creator: Rebecca Bellan
title: (No AI) ‘AI-pilled’ firms spend $7,500 per employee each month on AI
dc:creator: Rebecca Bellan
content: The most AI-obsessed firms are spending roughly $7,500 monthly per employee on AI, per Ramp AI Index. That's not more than an engineer's salary — yet.
contentSnippet: The most AI-obsessed firms are spending roughly $7,500 monthly per employee on AI, per Ramp AI Index. That's not more than an engineer's salary — yet.

creator: Russell Brandom
title: (No AI) How memory tools can make AI models worse
dc:creator: Russell Brandom
content: New research suggests that AI memory systems can degrade model performance and encourage sycophantic tendencies.
contentSnippet: New research suggests that AI memory systems can degrade model performance and encourage sycophantic tendencies.

title: (No AI) Microsoft, like, totally gets why students are booing AI-pilled graduation speakers
author: Mia Sato
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2025/11/STK095_MICROSOFT_CVirginia_A.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

New college graduates around the country have been [booing and heckling commencement speakers who hype up AI](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/935602/graduates-boo-ai-ceos). Microsoft would like everyone to talk it out.

In [a blog post](https://blogs.microsoft.com/on-the-issues/2026/06/10/ai-jobs-and-the-next-generation/) running more than 3,100 words, Microsoft vice chair and president Brad Smith addressed the recent spate of viral clips from graduation ceremonies, like former Google CEO Eric Schmidt [getting an earful at the University of Arizona](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=tNH43a1EI7s), or the speaker in Florida who [seemed surprised](https://www.youtube.com/shorts/ZCNgENc_0N0) when students booed at the mention of AI as "the next industrial revolution." The videos speak to a broader societal sentiment around AI - the technology [is deeply unpopular](https://www.theverge.com/ai-artificial-intelligence/891724/nbc-news-march-2026-poll-ai-ice) even as technology compani …

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/news/947831/college-speakers-booed-ai-microsoft)
contentSnippet:
New college graduates around the country have been booing and heckling commencement speakers who hype up AI. Microsoft would like everyone to talk it out.
In a blog post running more than 3,100 words, Microsoft vice chair and president Brad Smith addressed the recent spate of viral clips from graduation ceremonies, like former Google CEO Eric Schmidt getting an earful at the University of Arizona, or the speaker in Florida who seemed surprised when students booed at the mention of AI as "the next industrial revolution." The videos speak to a broader societal sentiment around AI - the technology is deeply unpopular even as technology compani …
Read the full story at The Verge.

In a blog post running more than 3,100 words, Microsoft vice chair and president Brad Smith addressed the recent spate of viral clips from graduation ceremonies, like former Google CEO […]

title: (No AI) The future of AI regulation is courting the strangest, most anxious bedfellows
author: Tina Nguyen
content:
![](https://platform.theverge.com/wp-content/uploads/sites/2/2026/06/gettyimages-2279697530.jpg?quality=90&strip=all&crop=0,0,100,100)

(L-R) Sen. Mike Rounds, Pamela Brown, Chris Malachowsky, Kevin O'Leary, Gabriele Caccia, Tammy Haddad, Michele L. Jawando, Sen. Mark Warner, Michael Kelly and Major General Patrick Ellis attend the Second Annual AI Honors. | Getty Images for Washington AI N

_Hello and welcome to_ Regulator_, a newsletter for_ Verge _subscribers about tech politics, tech influence, and tech shenanigans in Washington, DC. (If you're not a subscriber,_ [_you can get on board here_](https://www.theverge.com/subscribe)_.) We're back after a two-week hiatus, during most of which I was gallivanting in the Netherlands for a family wedding, and a trip to the Heineken Experience, which is, truly, an_ [_~experience~_](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=vYNgfwu1TPc)_._

_Before I left, I asked everyone in Washington to please chill out while I was gone. This clearly did not happen, and I have returned to a political landscape that can be best described as that_ [_meme from_ Community _where the room is on fire_](https://giphy.com/clips/erichamlet-LbUjyI9ye230n0mnSG)_. Let's get into th …_

[Read the full story at The Verge.](https://www.theverge.com/column/947838/washington-ai-network-honors-2026-midterms)

title: (No AI) From data to decisions: how LSEG is scaling trusted AI

title: (No AI) How engineers at Nextdoor use Codex to build without limits

title: (No AI) Fluid, natural voice translation with Gemini 3.5 Live Translate

creator: Dell Cameron
title: (No AI) Wrongful Arrest Exposes Failures in One of the Oldest Police Face-Recognition Tools in the US
dc:creator: Dell Cameron
content: The ACLU is suing two Florida police departments over the arrest of a Fort Myers man in a child-abduction case, saying officers treated a flawed face-recognition match as a near-certain ID.
contentSnippet: The ACLU is suing two Florida police departments over the arrest of a Fort Myers man in a child-abduction case, saying officers treated a flawed face-recognition match as a near-certain ID.

creator: Fernanda González
title: (No AI) China Opens World’s First Wind-Powered Underwater Data Center
dc:creator: Fernanda González
content: With an initial capacity of 24 megawatts, the innovative data center uses seawater as a natural cooling system.
contentSnippet: With an initial capacity of 24 megawatts, the innovative data center uses seawater as a natural cooling system.
